# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    git-config-health:
      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before committing."

    beads-tracker-health:
      run: "sh scripts/beads-tracker-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Beads tracker has unstaged changes. Run 'git add .ddx/beads.jsonl'."

    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
    # debug-go:
    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
    #   glob: "*.go"
    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
    #   run: |
    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     else
    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
        else
          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
        fi
      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"

    # Go formatting - native gofmt
    go-fmt:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Format"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
          exit 1
        fi
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go linting - golangci-lint required
    go-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Lint"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
          exit 1
        fi
        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
        # under analysistest's GOPATH-style loader.
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '/testdata/' || true)
        [ -z "$packages" ] && exit 0
        for pkg in $packages; do
          golangci-lint run --timeout=60s --new-from-rev=HEAD~1 "$pkg"
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go build - only for changed packages
    go-build:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Build"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: 'go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...'
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage for the
    # no-unbounded-prompts invariant. Scoped to packages containing
    # staged files, with diagnostics filtered to only those staged
    # files so existing violations in untouched packages don't block
    # unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical
    # sites; full-tree enforcement lives in the `ci` block below.
    evidence-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/evidencelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Unbounded prompt sink detected in staged file. Route through internal/evidence or add `// evidence:allow-unbounded reason=\"...\"`."

    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on
    # *Runtime structs and reintroduction of legacy *Options types.
    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics
    # filtered to only those staged files so existing violations in
    # untouched packages don't block unrelated commits. The full-tree
    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
    runtime-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Forbidden runtime-struct field or legacy *Options type in staged file. Move durable knobs to config.ResolvedConfig and use the *Runtime pair (SD-024 §Stage 4 / TD-024 §Lint rule)."

    # Go tests - targeted testing
    go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race -timeout 30m "./$pkg"
          fi
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      env:
        CGO_ENABLED: "1"
        SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS: "true"

    # Secrets scanning - prefer gitleaks when available
    secrets:
      <<: *security_checks
      name: "Scan for secrets"
      run: |
        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          gitleaks protect --staged --verbose --redact
        else
          # Fallback to staged-added-line pattern matching when gitleaks is absent.
          # Scanning whole tracker JSONL files causes noisy false positives on prose.
          added_lines=$(git diff --cached --unified=0 --no-color -- {staged_files} | grep '^+' | grep -v '^+++')

          patterns='((api[_-]?key|apikey|secret|password|passwd|pwd|credential|private[_-]?key|access[_-]?token|auth[_-]?token)[[:space:]]*[:=][[:space:]]*["'"'"'][A-Za-z0-9_./+=-]{20,})|(Authorization:[[:space:]]*Bearer[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9._-]{20,})'
          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E "$patterns"; then
            echo "Potential secrets detected"
            exit 1
          fi

          # AWS keys
          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}'; then
            echo "AWS key pattern detected"
            exit 1
          fi

          # GitHub tokens
          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}'; then
            echo "GitHub token pattern detected"
            exit 1
          fi
        fi
      exclude:
        - "*.md"
        - "go.sum"
        - "package-lock.json"
        - "*_test.go"
        - "**/dist/**"
        - "scripts/test-secrets-fallback.sh"
      fail_text: "Secrets detected! Remove them or add to .gitleaks.toml if false positive"

    # Binary and large file check
    large-files:
      name: "Check file sizes"

name: "Check file sizes"
      run: |
        for file in {staged_files}; do
          size=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$size" -gt 5242880 ]; then
            echo "Large file detected: $file ($(($size / 1048576))MB)"
            exit 1
          fi
        done
      exclude:
        - "*.png"
        - "*.jpg"
        - "*.jpeg"
        - "*.gif"
        - "*.svg"
        - "*.ico"
        - "*.pdf"
        - "*.woff"
        - "*.woff2"
        - "*.ttf"
        - "*.eot"
        # bead store grows monotonically with queue activity; expected to exceed 5MB
        - ".ddx/beads.jsonl"
        # archive partner accumulates closed beads forever; trends past 5MB
        - ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl"
      fail_text: "Large files detected. Use Git LFS for files over 5MB"

    # DDx validation - only when relevant files change
    ddx-validate:
      name: "Validate DDx"
      run: |
        # Only run if DDx files are staged
        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '(\.ddx\.yml|templates/|patterns/|prompts/)'; then
          exit 0
        fi

        # Strip git's local-env-vars before invoking ddx so any git commands
        # the ddx process spawns honor cmd.Dir / -C <dir> rather than the
        # GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / etc. inherited from this hook's context.
        # Without this, ddx's git invocations mutate THIS repo (the hook's),
        # producing core.bare=true and stray core.worktree corruption.
        # ddx-64ac553a Phase 0 (defensive triage; real fix is the central
        # git.Command wrapper in Phase 2a).
        unset $(git rev-parse --local-env-vars)

        # Collect staged paths and pass them to --paths so doctor only validates
        # the checks relevant to the staged files (config or resource), skipping
        # global installation checks (e.g. legacy skill symlinks) that are
        # unrelated to the content of the staged change. [ddx-777f4a09]
        staged_paths="$(git diff --cached --name-only | tr '\n' ' ')"

        # Check for DDx command availability
        if command -v ddx >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          ddx doctor --paths "$staged_paths" >/dev/null 2>&1
        elif [ -x "./cli/ddx" ]; then
          ./cli/ddx doctor --paths "$staged_paths" >/dev/null 2>&1
        fi
      fail_text: "DDx validation failed. Run 'ddx doctor' for details"

    skill-schema:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Validate skill metadata"
      run: |
        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^(library/skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md|cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md)$'; then
          exit 0
        fi
        make skill-schema
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    sync-embedded-default-plugin:
      name: "Sync cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library from library/"
      root: "cli/"
      run: |
        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^library/(skills/|package\.yaml$)'; then
          exit 0
        fi
        make copy-skills
        cd .. && git add cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/
      stage_fixed: true
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Failed to sync cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library from library/. Run 'cd cli && make copy-skills' manually and stage the result."

    design-md-lint:
      name: "DESIGN.md lint"
      glob: ".stitch/DESIGN.md"
      run: |
        if ! command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "bun not found; skipping DESIGN.md lint"
          exit 0
        fi
        bun install --frozen-lockfile 2>/dev/null || bun install
        bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md
      fail_text: "DESIGN.md lint failed. Run: bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md"

    test-engineer-persona-drift:
      name: "Guard test-engineer persona (ddx-482fad2f)"
      glob: "library/personas/test-engineer.md"
      run: |
        if grep -q "Mock all external dependencies" library/personas/test-engineer.md; then
          echo "ERROR: 'Mock all external dependencies' is forbidden in the test-engineer persona (see ddx-482fad2f)"
          exit 1
        fi
        if grep -q "test doubles (mocks" library/personas/test-engineer.md; then
          echo "ERROR: 'test doubles (mocks, stubs, spies)' lists mocks and stubs as equivalent — forbidden (see ddx-482fad2f)"
          exit 1
        fi
      fail_text: "test-engineer persona contains forbidden mock-promotion phrases"

    no-tmp-in-test-factory:
      name: "Guard NewCommandFactory(\"/tmp\") in tests (ddx-9063101c)"
      glob: "cli/cmd/*_test.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/cmd/[^/]+_test\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        bad=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs grep -lF 'NewCommandFactory("/tmp")' 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$bad" ]; then
          printf '%s\n' "$bad" | xargs grep -nF 'NewCommandFactory("/tmp")'
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "NewCommandFactory(\"/tmp\") found in cli/cmd/*_test.go — use t.TempDir() instead"

    skill-dir-anchor:
      name: "Skill dirs must have SKILL.md"
      run: |
        failed=0
        for base in .agents/skills .claude/skills; do
          [ -d "$base" ] || continue
          for d in $(ls -A "$base"); do
            full="$base/$d"
            [ -d "$full" ] || continue
            if [ ! -f "$full/SKILL.md" ]; then
              echo "ERROR: skill-dir $full has no SKILL.md; skill directories must be real skills with a SKILL.md anchor. If this is contamination, remove the directory. If this is a new skill, add a SKILL.md."
              failed=1
            fi
          done
        done
        exit $failed
      fail_text: "Skill directory missing SKILL.md. Either add SKILL.md (new skill) or remove the directory (contamination)."

# ====================
# CI/CD - Full validation (all files)
# ====================
ci:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Full conflict check
    conflicts-all:
      name: "Check all merge conflicts"
      run: |
        # Check for actual merge conflict markers (exactly 7 characters followed by space or newline)
        conflicts=$(find . -type f ! -path "./.git/*" ! -path "./cli/.git/*" ! -name "lefthook.yml" \
          ! -path "*/node_modules/*" ! -path "*/.svelte-kit/*" ! -path "./cli/internal/server/frontend/build/*" \
          ! -path "./website/public/*" ! -path "./cli/build/*" \
          -exec grep -l "^<<<<<<<[ \t]\|^=======\$\|^>>>>>>>[ \t]\|^||||||[ \t]" {} \; 2>/dev/null | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflicts" ]; then
          echo "Merge conflict markers found in: $conflicts"
          exit 1
        fi

    # Full secrets scan
    secrets-all:
      <<: *security_checks
      name: "Full secrets scan"
      run: |
        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          gitleaks detect --verbose --redact
        else
          echo "Consider installing gitleaks for comprehensive secret scanning"
        fi

    # Go full test suite
    go-test-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Full Go test suite"
      root: "cli/"
      run: 'go test -race -cover ./...'

    bead-schema:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Bead Schema Guard"
      run: 'make bead-schema'

    skill-schema:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Skill Metadata Guard"
      run: 'make skill-schema'

    # Go formatting check all files
    go-fmt-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Check all Go formatting"
      root: "cli/"
      run: |
        unformatted=$(gofmt -l .)
        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
          echo "Unformatted files: $unformatted"
          exit 1
        fi

    # Go lint all files
    go-lint-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Lint all Go files"
      root: "cli/"
      run: |
        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
          exit 1
        fi
        golangci-lint run --timeout=5m

    # Go build all targets
    go-build-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Build all targets"
      root: "cli/"
      run: 'go build -v ./...'

    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage. Repo-wide
    # invariant check; runs in CI even when no Go files were staged
    # in the triggering commit so historical regressions still surface.
    evidence-lint-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts) — full tree"
      root: "cli/"
      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint ./...'

    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: full-tree gate. The pre-commit hook
    # only checks staged-package diagnostics; this CI gate enforces the
    # post-cleanup invariant — zero forbidden runtime fields and zero
    # legacy *Options types anywhere in cli/. Activated now that the
    # RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions/ExecuteBeadOptions
    # retirement beads have all landed (ddx-ade5ebb3, ddx-a8eaef0a).
    runtime-lint-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options) — full tree"
      root: "cli/"
      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...'

    # FEAT-006 routing-lint: dead-code / anti-reintroduction gate for
    # the compensating DDx-side routing helpers retired by ddx-3bd7396a
    # (parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7, cleanup bead ddx-653f6ac9). Fails
    # CI if any of ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef,
    # AdaptiveMinTier, workersByHarness, or the retired --escalate /
    # --override-model / agent.routing.profile_ladders /
    # agent.routing.model_overrides flag/config tokens reappear in
    # cli/ Go source.
    routing-lint-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Routing Lint (FEAT-006 compensating-routing helpers retired) — full tree"
      root: "cli/"
      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...'

    docs-routing-lint:

data.text: There are `849` matching `.ddx` joins left. Before editing, I’m splitting that into production vs test hits; if the production side is already mostly clean, this bead stays in scope as the planned test sweep plus hook wiring.

input.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python - <<'PY'
import re, subprocess, collections
cmd = r'''rg -n \"filepath\\.Join\\(["'^)]*'"\\\"\\.ddx\\\"\" cli/ --type go | grep -v '"'^cli/internal/ddxroot/'"' '''
out = subprocess.check_output(['bash','-lc',cmd], text=True)
test=0
prod=0
files=collections.Counter()
for line in out.splitlines():
    path=line.split(':',1)[0]
    files[path]+=1
    if path.endswith('_test.go'):
        test+=1
    else:
        prod+=1
print('test_hits', test)
print('prod_hits', prod)
print('top_prod_files')
for path,count in files.items():
    if not path.endswith('_test.go'):
        print(count, path)
PY"

data.text:
Plan:
1. Extend `cli/internal/ddxroot` with explicit registry APIs and path helpers so both the new command and the `.ddx` audit have one place to route through.
2. Add `ddx project worktree list` and `ddx project worktree master <path>` plus tests that cover subsequent registration and operator re-pin behavior.
3. Sweep remaining `filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...)` callsites to those helpers, then wire the static grep into `lefthook.yml`.
4. Run the targeted Go tests first, then the required `lefthook run pre-commit`, and commit once the tree is green.

package cmd

import (
	"bytes"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/persona"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestPersonaCLI_Lifecycle verifies the new CLI subcommands — new, edit,
// fork, delete — against AC #4 (CLI parity): project-local files get
// created/updated/deleted, and library personas are protected.
func TestPersonaCLI_Lifecycle(t *testing.T) {
	workDir := t.TempDir()
	libraryRoot := filepath.Join(workDir, "library")
	libPersonasDir := filepath.Join(libraryRoot, "personas")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(libPersonasDir, 0o755))

	libPersona := `---
name: architect
roles: [architect]
description: Library architect
tags: []
---

# Architect
`
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(libPersonasDir, "architect.md"), []byte(libPersona), 0o644))

	cfg := "version: \"2.0\"\nlibrary:\n  path: library\n"
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(cfg), 0o644))

	bodyFile := filepath.Join(workDir, "body.md")
	bodyContent := `---
name: ignored
roles: [code-reviewer]
description: Our reviewer
tags: []
---

# Our Reviewer
`
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(bodyFile, []byte(bodyContent), 0o644))

	run := func(args ...string) (string, error) {
		rootCmd := getPersonaIntegrationTestRootCommand(workDir)
		var out bytes.Buffer
		rootCmd.SetOut(&out)
		rootCmd.SetErr(&out)
		rootCmd.SetArgs(args)
		err := rootCmd.Execute()
		return out.String(), err
	}

	// new: creates a project-local persona.
	out, err := run("persona", "new", "our-reviewer", "--body", bodyFile)
	require.NoError(t, err, out)
	projectFile := filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx", "personas", "our-reviewer.md")
	assert.FileExists(t, projectFile)

	// edit: updates via --body.
	out, err = run("persona", "edit", "our-reviewer", "--body", bodyFile)
	require.NoError(t, err, out)

	// fork: copies the library persona into the project dir.
	out, err = run("persona", "fork", "architect", "--as", "architect-local")
	require.NoError(t, err, out)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx", "personas", "architect-local.md"))

	// edit: library persona is rejected.
	_, err = run("persona", "edit", "architect", "--body", bodyFile)
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "library")

	// delete: library persona is rejected.
	_, err = run("persona", "delete", "architect")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "library")

	// delete: project-local succeeds.
	out, err = run("persona", "delete", "our-reviewer")
	require.NoError(t, err, out)
	_, err = os.Stat(projectFile)
	assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(err))
}

// TestPersonaCLI_GraphQLParity covers AC #4: a persona created via `ddx
// persona new` is visible to the persona package's loader, proving that
// the CLI and GraphQL paths share the same storage.
func TestPersonaCLI_GraphQLParity(t *testing.T) {
	workDir := t.TempDir()
	libraryRoot := filepath.Join(workDir, "library")
	libPersonasDir := filepath.Join(libraryRoot, "personas")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(libPersonasDir, 0o755))
	libPersona := "---\nname: architect\nroles: [architect]\ndescription: Library architect\ntags: []\n---\n\n# Architect\n"
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(libPersonasDir, "architect.md"), []byte(libPersona), 0o644))

	cfg := "version: \"2.0\"\nlibrary:\n  path: library\n"
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(cfg), 0o644))

	bodyFile := filepath.Join(workDir, "body.md")
	body := "---\nname: ignored\nroles: [code-reviewer]\ndescription: Shared reviewer\ntags: []\n---\n\n# Shared\n"
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(bodyFile, []byte(body), 0o644))

	rootCmd := getPersonaIntegrationTestRootCommand(workDir)
	var buf bytes.Buffer
	rootCmd.SetOut(&buf)
	rootCmd.SetErr(&buf)
	rootCmd.SetArgs([]string{"persona", "new", "shared-reviewer", "--body", bodyFile})
	require.NoError(t, rootCmd.Execute(), buf.String())

	loader := persona.NewPersonaLoader(workDir)
	all, err := loader.ListPersonas()
	require.NoError(t, err)

	names := map[string]string{}
	for _, p := range all {
		names[p.Name] = p.Source
	}
	assert.Equal(t, persona.SourceProject, names["shared-reviewer"],
		"CLI-created persona should show up via the persona loader as a project persona")
	assert.Equal(t, persona.SourceLibrary, names["architect"],
		"library architect still visible with library source")
}

func TestPersonaCLI_LoadUsesProjectOverride(t *testing.T) {
	workDir := t.TempDir()
	libraryRoot := filepath.Join(workDir, "library")
	libPersonasDir := filepath.Join(libraryRoot, "personas")
	projectPersonasDir := filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx", "personas")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(libPersonasDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(projectPersonasDir, 0o755))

	cfg := `version: "2.0"
library:
  path: library

func (f *CommandFactory) warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd *cobra.Command) {
	if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
		return
	}
	if f.Commit == "" || f.Commit == "unknown" {
		return
	}

	repoRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
	if repoRoot == "" || !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoRoot) {
		return
	}

	originURL, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "remote", "get-url", "origin")
	if err != nil || !isDDXOriginURL(originURL) {
		return
	}

data.text: I found one real semantic regression from the rewrite: a couple of workspace-discovery loops started calling the bootstrap path helper, which would create state while merely searching. I’m correcting those before I trust the full test run.
The grep gate and command path are both in place. I’m moving to full verification now: first `cd cli && go test ./...`, then `lefthook run pre-commit` on the staged change set.

data.text: The next failures split into two classes: ordinary missing imports, and one real cycle where `internal/git` cannot import `internal/ddxroot` because `ddxroot` already depends on `git`. I’m fixing the cycle by using a local non-literal segment there, and adding the remaining imports explicitly where `goimports` was insufficient.

`goimports` from the repo root wasn’t resolving the `cli` module path correctly. I’m rerunning it from inside `cli/`, where it can see the module and add the missing `internal/ddxroot` imports properly.

data.text: The module-wide test run is past the import/cycle stage and compiling now. I’m letting that continue, then I’ll handle any remaining behavioral failures before I stage and run the hook gate.

The first real failures are from tests that assumed “no `.ddx` means error or in-tree bootstrap.” Under the new root-resolution contract that’s no longer true, and those tests also need an isolated `XDG_DATA_HOME` instead of silently writing to the user’s real home. I’m tracing the test helpers that should own that environment rather than patching individual assertions blindly.

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func newBeadTestRoot(t *testing.T, workingDir string) *CommandFactory {
	t.Helper()
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_DIR", "")
	return NewCommandFactory(workingDir)
}

func TestBeadCommandsCRUDLifecycle(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Fix auth bug", "--type", "bug", "--priority", "1", "--labels", "backend,urgent", "--acceptance", "bug is fixed")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	createdID := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, createdID)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ddxroot.DirName, "beads.jsonl"))

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", createdID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var created map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &created))
	assert.Equal(t, createdID, created["id"])
	assert.Equal(t, "Fix auth bug", created["title"])
	assert.Equal(t, "bug", created["issue_type"])
	assert.Equal(t, "open", created["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), created["priority"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", createdID, "--status", "in_progress", "--assignee", "me", "--labels", "backend")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	updatedOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", createdID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var updated map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(updatedOut), &updated))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", updated["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "me", updated["owner"])
	require.Len(t, updated["labels"], 1)

	listOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "list", "--status", "in_progress", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var listed []map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(listOut), &listed))
	require.Len(t, listed, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, createdID, listed[0]["id"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "close", createdID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	statusOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "status", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var status map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(statusOut), &status))
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["total"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["closed"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(0), status["open"])
}

func TestBeadUpdateStatusUsesLifecycleTransition(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Transition target", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--status", "blocked")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "transition_requires_external_blocker")

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--status", "in_progress", "--assignee", "me")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var got map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &got))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", got["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "me", got["owner"])
}

func TestBeadCommandsClaimUsesExplicitAssignee(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Claim me", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--claim", "--assignee", "alice")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var bead map[string]any

assert.Equal(t, float64(2), status["total"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["open"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["closed"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["ready"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(0), status["blocked"])
}

func TestBeadBlockedExcludesDependencyWaiting(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	depRootOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Dep root", "--priority", "1")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	depRootID := strings.TrimSpace(depRootOut)

	depBlockedOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Dep blocked child", "--priority", "2")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	depBlockedID := strings.TrimSpace(depBlockedOut)

	parkedOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Retry parked", "--priority", "0")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	parkedID := strings.TrimSpace(parkedOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "dep", "add", depBlockedID, depRootID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(workingDir, ddxroot.DirName))
	until := time.Now().UTC().Add(3 * time.Hour).Truncate(time.Second)
	require.NoError(t, store.SetExecutionCooldown(parkedID, until, "no_changes", "agent made no commits", ""))

	externalOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "External blocked", "--priority", "3")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	externalID := strings.TrimSpace(externalOut)
	require.NoError(t, store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(externalID, bead.StatusBlocked, bead.LifecycleTransitionOptions{
		ExternalBlockerReason: "waiting on upstream release",
		Reason:                "test external blocker",
		Source:                "test",
	}, nil))

	blockedJSON, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "blocked", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var entries []map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(blockedJSON), &entries))
	require.Len(t, entries, 1, "only status=blocked external blockers should surface")

data.text: The remaining failures are concentrated in tests and one likely real behavior regression around reviewer-stream artifact paths. I’m inspecting that function first, because if it’s genuinely returning host-relative paths instead of the stable `.ddx/executions/...` convention, that should be fixed in code rather than papered over in tests.

data.output:
package metric

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"sync"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	ddxexec "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/exec"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func writeMetricArtifact(t *testing.T, wd, id string) {
	t.Helper()
	path := filepath.Join(wd, "docs", "metrics", id+".md")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755))
	content := "---\nddx:\n  id: " + id + "\n---\n# " + id + "\n"
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0o644))
}

func writeArtifact(t *testing.T, wd, dir, id string) {
	t.Helper()
	path := filepath.Join(wd, "docs", dir, id+".md")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755))
	content := "---\nddx:\n  id: " + id + "\n---\n# " + id + "\n"
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0o644))
}

func writeMetricDefinition(t *testing.T, wd string, def Definition) {
	t.Helper()
	store := ddxexec.NewStore(wd)
	require.NoError(t, store.SaveDefinition(ddxexec.Definition{
		ID:          def.DefinitionID,
		ArtifactIDs: []string{def.MetricID},
		Executor: ddxexec.ExecutorSpec{
			Kind:    ddxexec.ExecutorKindCommand,
			Command: append([]string{}, def.Command...),
			Cwd:     def.Cwd,
			Env:     cloneStringMap(def.Env),
		},
		Result: ddxexec.ResultSpec{
			Metric: &ddxexec.MetricResultSpec{
				Unit: def.Thresholds.Unit,
			},
		},
		Evaluation: ddxexec.Evaluation{
			Comparison: def.Comparison,
			Thresholds: ddxexec.Thresholds{
				WarnMS:    def.Thresholds.Warn,
				RatchetMS: def.Thresholds.Ratchet,
			},
		},
		Active:    def.Active,
		CreatedAt: def.CreatedAt,
	}))
}

func TestValidateRunAndHistory(t *testing.T) {
	wd := t.TempDir()
	writeMetricArtifact(t, wd, "MET-001")
	writeMetricDefinition(t, wd, Definition{
		DefinitionID: "metric-startup-time@1",
		MetricID:     "MET-001",
		Command:      []string{"sh", "-c", "printf '14.6ms\\n'"},
		Thresholds:   Thresholds{Warn: 20, Ratchet: 30, Unit: "ms"},
		Comparison:   ComparisonLowerIsBetter,
		Active:       true,
		CreatedAt:    mustTime(t, "2026-04-04T15:00:00Z"),
	})

	store := NewStore(wd)
	def, doc, err := store.Validate("MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, "metric-startup-time@1", def.DefinitionID)
	require.Equal(t, "MET-001", doc.ID)

	rec, err := store.Run(context.Background(), "MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, StatusPass, rec.Status)
	assert.InDelta(t, 14.6, rec.Value, 0.01)
	assert.Equal(t, "ms", rec.Unit)
	assert.Equal(t, "MET-001", rec.ArtifactID)

	history, err := store.History("MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, history, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, rec.RunID, history[0].RunID)
}

func TestDefinitionAndHistoryPreferMetricArtifactID(t *testing.T) {
	wd := t.TempDir()
	writeMetricArtifact(t, wd, "MET-001")
	writeArtifact(t, wd, "misc", "DOC-001")
	writeMetricDefinition(t, wd, Definition{
		DefinitionID: "metric-startup-time@1",
		MetricID:     "MET-001",
		Command:      []string{"sh", "-c", "printf '14.6ms\\n'"},
		Thresholds:   Thresholds{Warn: 20, Ratchet: 30, Unit: "ms"},
		Comparison:   ComparisonLowerIsBetter,
		Active:       true,
		CreatedAt:    mustTime(t, "2026-04-04T15:00:00Z"),
	})

	store := NewStore(wd)
	def, err := store.LoadDefinition("MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "MET-001", def.MetricID)

	rec, err := store.Run(context.Background(), "MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "MET-001", rec.ArtifactID)
	assert.Equal(t, "MET-001", rec.MetricID)

	history, err := store.History("MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, history, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, "MET-001", history[0].MetricID)
	assert.Equal(t, "MET-001", history[0].ArtifactID)
}

func TestCompareAndTrend(t *testing.T) {
	wd := t.TempDir()
	writeMetricArtifact(t, wd, "MET-001")
	writeMetricDefinition(t, wd, Definition{
		DefinitionID: "metric-startup-time@1",
		MetricID:     "MET-001",
		Command:      []string{"sh", "-c", "printf '20ms\\n'"},
		Thresholds:   Thresholds{Warn: 20, Ratchet: 30, Unit: "ms"},
		Comparison:   ComparisonLowerIsBetter,
		Active:       true,
		CreatedAt:    mustTime(t, "2026-04-04T15:00:00Z"),
	})

	store := NewStore(wd)
	firstRec, err := store.Run(context.Background(), "MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	writeMetricDefinition(t, wd, Definition{
		DefinitionID: "metric-startup-time@1",
		MetricID:     "MET-001",
		Command:      []string{"sh", "-c", "printf '14.6ms\\n'"},
		Thresholds:   Thresholds{Warn: 20, Ratchet: 30, Unit: "ms"},
		Comparison:   ComparisonLowerIsBetter,
		Active:       true,
		CreatedAt:    mustTime(t, "2026-04-04T15:01:00Z"),
	})
	secondRec, err := store.Run(context.Background(), "MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	latest, result, err := store.Compare("MET-001", "baseline")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, secondRec.RunID, latest.RunID)
	assert.Equal(t, 20.0, result.Baseline)
	assert.InDelta(t, -5.4, result.Delta, 0.01)

	trend, err := store.Trend("MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 2, trend.Count)
	assert.InDelta(t, 14.6, trend.Latest, 0.01)
	assert.InDelta(t, 17.3, trend.Average, 0.01)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, firstRec.RunID)
}

func TestCompareAndTrendRejectMixedUnitsAndGroupHistoryByUnit(t *testing.T) {
	wd := t.TempDir()
	writeMetricArtifact(t, wd, "MET-001")
	writeMetricDefinition(t, wd, Definition{
		DefinitionID: "metric-startup-time@1",
		MetricID:     "MET-001",
		Command:      []string{"sh", "-c", "printf '20ms\\n'"},
		Thresholds:   Thresholds{Warn: 20, Ratchet: 30, Unit: "ms"},
		Comparison:   ComparisonLowerIsBetter,
		Active:       true,
		CreatedAt:    mustTime(t, "2026-04-04T15:00:00Z"),
	})

	store := NewStore(wd)
	_, err := store.Run(context.Background(), "MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	writeMetricDefinition(t, wd, Definition{
		DefinitionID: "metric-startup-time@2",
		MetricID:     "MET-001",
		Command:      []string{"sh", "-c", "printf '0.01USD\\n'"},
		Thresholds:   Thresholds{Warn: 20, Ratchet: 30, Unit: "USD"},
		Comparison:   ComparisonLowerIsBetter,
		Active:       true,
		CreatedAt:    mustTime(t, "2026-04-04T15:01:00Z"),
	})
	_, err = store.Run(context.Background(), "MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	_, _, err = store.Compare("MET-001", "baseline")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, strings.ToLower(err.Error()), "mixed units")

	_, err = store.Trend("MET-001")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, strings.ToLower(err.Error()), "mixed units")

	groups, err := store.GroupedHistory("MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, groups, 2)
	assert.Equal(t, "ms", groups[0].Unit)
	assert.Equal(t, "USD", groups[1].Unit)
	require.Len(t, groups[0].Records, 1)
	require.Len(t, groups[1].Records, 1)
}

func TestConcurrentHistoryWrites(t *testing.T) {
	wd := t.TempDir()
	writeMetricArtifact(t, wd, "MET-001")
	writeMetricDefinition(t, wd, Definition{
		DefinitionID: "metric-startup-time@1",
		MetricID:     "MET-001",
		Command:      []string{"sh", "-c", "printf '14.6ms\\n'"},
		Thresholds:   Thresholds{Warn: 20, Ratchet: 30, Unit: "ms"},
		Comparison:   ComparisonLowerIsBetter,
		Active:       true,
		CreatedAt:    mustTime(t, "2026-04-04T15:00:00Z"),
	})

	store := NewStore(wd)
	const writers = 12
	var wg sync.WaitGroup
	errCh := make(chan error, writers)
	for i := 0; i < writers; i++ {
		wg.Add(1)
		go func(i int) {
			defer wg.Done()
			_, err := store.Run(context.Background(), "MET-001")
			errCh <- err
		}(i)
	}
	wg.Wait()
	close(errCh)
	for err := range errCh {
		require.NoError(t, err)
	}

	raw, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(wd, ddxroot.DirName, "exec-runs.jsonl"))
	require.NoError(t, err)
	lines := 0
	for _, b := range raw {
		if b == '\n' {
			lines++
		}
	}
	assert.Equal(t, writers, lines)

	history, err := store.History("MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Len(t, history, writers)
}

func mustTime(t *testing.T, value string) time.Time {
	t.Helper()
	parsed, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, value)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	return parsed
}

// TestLegacyMetricsDirIgnored verifies that a pre-exec .ddx/metrics/ directory
// left over from a pre-release build does not cause DDx to crash. The current
// metric store delegates to the exec substrate and never reads .ddx/metrics/.
func TestLegacyMetricsDirIgnored(t *testing.T) {
	wd := t.TempDir()

	// Simulate old-format .ddx/metrics/ data written by the pre-exec metric store.
	metricsDir := filepath.Join(wd, ddxroot.DirName, "metrics")
	defsDir := filepath.Join(metricsDir, "definitions")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(defsDir, 0o755))

	oldDef := `{"definition_id":"metric-startup-time@1","metric_id":"MET-001","command":["sh","-c","echo 10ms"],"active":true,"created_at":"2026-04-04T10:00:00Z"}`
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(defsDir, "metric-startup-time@1.json"), []byte(oldDef), 0o644))

Parent bead ddx-06cbaa90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/serv
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster asserts later worktrees register but do not overwrite master.
- TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin asserts `ddx project worktree master <path>` updates the pin.
- Static check via lefthook: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. The lint rule is wired into pre-commit.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. The Fizeau repo root has five top-level dirs/files related to benchmarking with inconsistent naming:
- bench/ — corpus + README.md + (formerly) empty results/
- bench-fixed — was an aarch64 binary at root; now removed (e8855c08)
- benchmark — bash script (5KB) at root
- benchmark-results/ — 4.4GB output dir; now gitignored (e8855c08)
- benchscore/ — Go package (score.go + score_test.go), already in .gitignore

A new contributor cannot tell which is source, which is data, which is a wrapper script, and which is build output. The naming clusters semi-meaningfully but inconsistently — bench- prefix mixes with benchmark- prefix.

PROPOSED FIX
Pick one canonical naming scheme and migrate:
- Option A (preferred): bench → bench/corpus + bench/README.md; benchscore → bench/scorer/; benchmark script → bench/run (the script); benchmark-results stays as output dir but rename to bench/results — already gitignored. One top-level `bench/` directory containing source (scorer), data (corpus), runner (run), and output (results/, .gitignored).
- Option B: keep separate top-level dirs but rename to consistent prefix: bench-corpus/, bench-scorer/, bench-runner.sh, bench-results/. More files at root.

NON-SCOPE
- Don't change the benchmark *behavior* — same scripts, same output formats, same scorer logic.
- Don't change benchmark-results retention (separate concern, already gitignored).
- Don't rewrite the corpus (separate bead if ever needed).

DEPS
- Don't start until the harness-interface-refactor drain completes. Same merge-conflict concern as the root .go refactor.

RISK
Low. The bench cluster is loosely coupled — the scorer is its own Go package, the corpus is YAML/JSON data, the runner is a shell script. The riskiest piece is the runner referring to old paths; update all references.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. Single top-level `bench/` directory after the consolidation (with corpus/, scorer/, README.md, run script). Or — if Option B is chosen — consistent prefix across all bench-* top-level entries.
2. All scripts that reference the old paths are updated (search the repo for `bench-fixed`, `benchscore`, `benchmark-results`, and standalone `benchmark` script).

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.

Parent bead ddx-06cbaa90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/serv
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster asserts later worktrees register but do not overwrite master.
- TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin asserts `ddx project worktree master <path>` updates the pin.
- Static check via lefthook: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. The lint rule is wired into pre-commit.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. 317123 /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/codex exec --json --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -C /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e4c3d791-20260515T070047-706bc975 -m gpt-5.4 <execute-bead>   <instructions> You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-e4c3d791">     <title>ddx home: presence-based root resolution + per-project worktree registry: part 4</title>     <description> PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-06cbaa90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/serv
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster asserts later worktrees register but do not overwrite master.
- TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin asserts `ddx project worktree master <path>` updates the pin.
- Static check via lefthook: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. The lint rule is wired into pre-commit.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. 3. Static check via lefthook: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. Implemented and committed as `bcedc270f`.

The acceptance slice is covered by the new worktree CLI in [project.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e4c3d791-20260515T070047-706bc975/cli/cmd/project.go), the operator re-pin test in [project_worktree_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e4c3d791-20260515T070047-706bc975/cli/cmd/project_worktree_test.go), the subsequent non-master registration test in [ddxroot_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e4c3d791-20260515T070047-706bc975/cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot_test.go), and the pre-commit path audit wired in [lefthook.yml](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e4c3d791-20260515T070047-706bc975/lefthook.yml). The `cli/` sweep removed hardcoded `.ddx` path joins outside `cli/internal/ddxroot/`.

Verification passed via `lefthook run pre-commit`, and the commit hook reran the full gated suite, including the package-by-package `go test -short -race` pass.
